assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md explicitly states: 'Ecosystem: **RPM**', 'Vulnerable package: openssl-libs (RPM system package)', 'Lock file: `rpms.lock.yaml`', and 'Investigation method: Lock file inspection (rpms.lock.yaml is configured in Ecosystem Mappings)'. The ecosystem is correctly identified as RPM based on the library name and Ecosystem Mappings config, with no mention of Cargo.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a table with all five 2.2.x versions: 2.2.0 (v0.4.5, 3.0.7-25.el9_3), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8, 3.0.7-27.el9_4), 2.2.2 (v0.4.9, retag of 2.2.1), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11, 3.0.7-28.el9_4), and 2.2.4 (v0.4.12, 3.0.7-28.el9_4). The column header is 'openssl-libs (rpms.lock.yaml)', confirming rpms.lock.yaml as the data source for version extraction.

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context identifies openssl-libs as present in rpms.lock.yaml and uses this to determine the package origin — the lock file presence is the primary classification signal (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md section 'Dependency Chain (Step 2.3.5)' states: 'rpms.lock.yaml: present --> explicit install' and 'Origin: explicit install -- openssl-libs is present in `rpms.lock.yaml` at the pinned build tags for all 2.2.x versions, confirming it is an explicitly managed package (not inherited from the base image).' The lock file presence is clearly the primary classification signal.

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md states for 2.2.x: 'No new remediation task is required for this stream' (already fixed in 2.2.3+). For 2.1.x cross-stream: 'Since openssl-libs is an RPM system package present in rpms.lock.yaml (explicit install), one task is created for the Konflux release repo.' Only a single Jira task is created targeting rhtpa-release.0.3.z. There is no two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation pattern.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md task description contains all required sections: '## Repository' (rhtpa-release.0.3.z), '## Target Branch' (main), '## Description' (CVE remediation details), '## Implementation Notes' (update instructions for rpms.lock.yaml), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (three checkboxes). There is no 'Files to Modify' section, which is the correct omission per remediation-templates.md for system package paths.

assertion_results.text: The version impact output addresses SBOM verification in the dependency chain context — either noting that verification was skipped because cosign is not available or because external tools are prohibited, or presenting the SBOM classification result (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md in the Dependency Chain section explicitly states: 'SBOM verification: skipped -- cosign not available' and further elaborates: '**SBOM verification**: cosign is not available in this environment. SBOM comparison between final container image and base image was skipped. Using rpms.lock.yaml classification only.' This directly addresses the SBOM verification requirement by noting cosign unavailability.
